title: Human CSF Processing for 10x scRNA-seq
author: Le Zhang
materials: 10x Genomics Chromium Single Cell 5' Reagent Kit v2

steps.title: Collect the cerebrospinal Fluid (CSF) Room temperature (64-77°F / 18-25°C)
steps.instruction: Collect the cerebrospinal Fluid (CSF) Room temperature (64-77°F / 18-25°C). No fasting is required. Once collected, keep samples at On ice or at 4 °C .

steps.title: Perform lumbar puncture (LP) utilizing Sprotte...
steps.instruction: Perform lumbar puncture (LP) utilizing Sprotte atraumatic lumbar needle with cannula. In case of bleeding at the puncture site, collect the first 1-2 mL of CSF fluid in the CSF WASTE tube.

steps.title: Continue collecting CSF fluid in the...
steps.instruction: Continue collecting CSF fluid in the CSF WASTE tube until CSF is visibly devoid of red blood cells (RBC) contamination, i.e., no pinkish or red color. For the CSF sample, collect 30 mL of CSF fluid into pre-labeled tubes. Proceed with the next steps even if less than 30 mL of CSF was collected. Replace caps after CSF sample collection and mix gently by inverting each tube 3-4 times. CSF WASTE tube may be discarded.

steps.title: Record the total CSF volume and...
steps.instruction: Record the total CSF volume and if CSF sample looks grossly or visibly contaminated with RBC and/or if it is hued pink or red. Record the date and time of the CSF sample collected.

steps.title: Transfer the CSF sample as quickly...
steps.instruction: Transfer the CSF sample as quickly as possible to the lab. Then proceed to CSF centrifugation and processing.

steps.title: Spin down the CSF cells at...
steps.instruction: Spin down the CSF cells at 300 x g, 4 °C, 10 minutes using a swing bucket centrifuge.

steps.title: Aspirate the supernatant with a pipette,...
steps.instruction: Aspirate the supernatant with a pipette, leaving ~ 500 µL without disturbing the cell pellet. Save the CSF supernatant On ice for further aliquots.

steps.title: Mix and count the total CSF cell number
steps.instruction: Mix and count the total CSF cell number. Use 5 µL of the sample with 1:1 dilution of trypan blue and a C-Chip disposable hemacytometer (DHCN015). Record the total CSF cell number.

steps.title: Transfer to a 1
steps.instruction: Transfer to a 1.5-mL Eppendorf tube and spin down at 300 x g, 4 °C, 10 minutes using a swing bucket centrifuge.

steps.title: Carefully aspirate the supernatant, leaving ~ 50 µL
steps.instruction: Carefully aspirate the supernatant, leaving ~ 50 µL .

steps.title: Resuspend the cells and count
steps.instruction: Resuspend the cells and count.

steps.title: If the cells need to be...
steps.instruction: If the cells need to be diluted more, use the CSF supernatant.

steps.title: If there are leftover CSF cells...
steps.instruction: If there are leftover CSF cells after 10x loading, freeze down the cells.

steps.title: Submit for 10x single cell loading
steps.instruction: Submit for 10x single cell loading. Ideally 700-1200 cells/ul. If too few cells, submit all the sample of 50 µL .

steps.title: Use 10x Genomics Chromium Single Cell...
steps.instruction: Use 10x Genomics Chromium Single Cell 5' Reagent Kit v2 chemistry for single cell gene expression and TCR/BCR sequencing.

steps.title: Spin down the CSF cells at...
steps.instruction: Spin down the CSF cells at 400 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes using a swing bucket centrifuge.

steps.title: Carefully aspirate the supernatant
steps.instruction: Carefully aspirate the supernatant.

steps.title: Resuspend the cell pellet in 0
steps.instruction: Resuspend the cell pellet in 0.1 mL of Bambanker cell freezing medium.

steps.title: Freeze in Mr
steps.instruction: Freeze in Mr. Frosty freezing container in -80 °C freezer. Transfer to liquid N2 tank after 24h - 72h.

steps.title: Transfer to liquid N2 tank after 24h - 72h
steps.instruction: Transfer to liquid N2 tank after 24h - 72h.
